title: hieracium laevigatum subsp. nivale
intro: Hieracium laevigatum subsp. nivale is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, known for its hairy leaves and white or pinkish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and aiding digestion. Hieracium laevigatum subsp. nivale can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
